name: Grafbase GraphQL Platform API
what_it_does: GraphQL platform and API gateway that federates multiple GraphQL and REST APIs into a single unified graph — with schema registry, edge caching, authentication, and rate limiting deployed as a managed edge service.
use_cases: Federating multiple GraphQL APIs into a unified schema for agent consumption
Adding authentication, rate limiting, and caching to existing GraphQL APIs
Building serverless GraphQL resolvers that query multiple data sources
Deploying schema-first GraphQL APIs with automatic OpenAPI/REST bridge
Managing GraphQL schema registry across multiple teams and services
not_for: REST-only API development without GraphQL requirements
Simple single-service APIs that don't need federation
best_when: An agent needs to query multiple data sources through a single unified GraphQL API with edge-level performance and built-in auth.
avoid_when: You need REST-only APIs or are satisfied with direct service-level GraphQL without federation.

auth.notes: API key for management API. JWT/OIDC for gateway auth. Multiple auth providers supported (Auth0, Clerk, Cognito). Per-field and per-type authorization rules in schema. Edge-level auth evaluation.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $29/month; Team from $99/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Competitive free tier for development and small production. Request-based pricing. Edge deployment included at all tiers. Gateway mode available for connecting existing GraphQL APIs.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limiting configurable at schema level; plan limits on requests/month

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Developer documentation covers schema definition, resolvers, and federation. GraphQL introspection enabled. Error responses follow GraphQL error spec with extensions for detailed info. CLI for schema deployment.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All GraphQL queries are idempotent reads. Schema deployments are idempotent by schema hash. Mutations depend on resolver implementation.
known_agent_gotchas: Schema deployment propagation takes 30-60 seconds at edge — agent must wait before querying
Type merging for federation requires careful schema design to avoid conflicts
Edge caching may return stale data — use cache-control directives appropriately
Grafbase CLI required for local development — different from production edge behavior

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. TLS enforced at edge. Schema-level authorization rules. Multiple auth provider support. Edge deployment reduces attack surface. Query depth and complexity limiting available.
